steps.title: Identify the sufficiency threshold
steps.content: The passage states that wintering honeybees' maximum positive charge is “sufficiently high” to enable electrostatic pollen transfer; the graph shows that red mason bees have a much higher maximum charge than wintering honeybees.

steps.title: Apply the threshold to other species
steps.content:
Red mason bees show a maximum charge of about 780 pC, clearly above the wintering honeybee charge that the passage identifies as sufficient for electrostatic pollen transfer; a higher charge should produce the same effect.

The other choices either misrepresent the data, make unsupported claims, or draw conclusions beyond what the data supports.

That confirms D is correct.
